title: Ser vs estar: first contrast
subtitle: Separate identity from state or place.
overview: Use ser for what something is.
Use estar for where it is or how it is right now.

sections.title: Tiny contrasts
rows: The coffee is good in general.
The coffee is cold right now.

items.en: I am a student, but I am tired.

items.es: La tienda está cerca y es barata.
items.en: The store is nearby and it is cheap.

items.es: Mi casa es pequeña, pero está en una buena zona.
items.en: My house is small, but it is in a good area.
